Description: Column with all items renders everything immediately; LazyColumn recycles and renders only visible items with minimal overhead
Do: Use LazyColumn with items() or itemsIndexed(); provide key lambda for stable identity; avoid heavy composables as direct children
Don't: Use Column(content={ items.forEach{item->ItemRow(it)} }) for long lists; repeat items without key identity
Code Good: LazyColumn { items(items = posts, key = { it.id }) { post -> PostCard(post = post) } }
Code Bad: Column { posts.forEach { post -> PostCard(post = post) } }  // All items composed at once OOM risk

Guideline: Minimize recomposition with remember and derivedStateOf
Description: Composables recompose when read state changes; without derivedStateOf computed values trigger recomposition even when output hasn't changed
Do: Use remember for initialization; derivedStateOf for computed values from state; rememberSaveable for survive-recreation values
Don't: Create new object instances in composable without remember; use state.value directly without derivedStateOf when derived value is same
Code Good: val isScrolled by remember { derivedStateOf { listState.firstVisibleItemIndex > 0 } }  // Only recomposes when scrolled changes
Code Bad: val isScrolled = listState.firstVisibleItemIndex > 0  // Recomposes every scroll event

Description: Compose uses type stability to decide if recomposition is needed; unstable types (List Map interface) trigger unnecessary recompositions
Do: Use data classes for UI state; wrap unstable collections in @Immutable; use ImmutableList from kotlinx.collections.immutable
Don't: Pass raw List<T> directly as composable parameter (unstable); use non-data class with var fields as state
Code Good: @Immutable data class UiState(val posts: ImmutableList<Post>, val isLoading: Boolean)
Code Bad: data class UiState(val posts: List<Post>, val isLoading: Boolean)  // List is unstable triggers full recomposition

Guideline: Use Navigation Compose with type-safe routes (Compose 1.8+ / NavController)
Description: Manual route string matching is error-prone; type-safe routes using @Serializable data classes prevent typos and provide compile-time safety
Do: Use NavController with type-safe NavDestination (@Serializable objects/classes); NavGraphBuilder DSL; popUpTo for correct back stack
Don't: Navigate by raw string 'home/{userId}' with manual parsing; use deprecated FragmentManager with Compose
Code Good: @Serializable data class Profile(val userId: String); navController.navigate(Profile(userId=id)); composable<Profile> { back -> val args = back.toRoute<Profile>(); ProfileScreen(args.userId) }

Description: Compose screens need to intercept system back press for custom behavior; without BackHandler system back navigates out of scope
Do: Use BackHandler(enabled=condition) composable; disableDefault with enabled=false when not needed; prefer system back behavior when possible
Don't: Override Activity.onBackPressed instead of BackHandler in Compose; always enable BackHandler blocking all navigation (memory leak)
Code Good: BackHandler(enabled = isSearchActive) { isSearchActive = false }  // Only intercepts when search is open
Code Bad: // In Activity: override fun onBackPressed() { /* custom */ }  // Doesn't work with Compose navigation

Description: Compose edge-to-edge requires consuming WindowInsets for nav bar status bar; Scaffold handles most cases but custom layouts need explicit inset handling
Do: Call WindowCompat.setDecorFitsSystemWindows(window,false) in Activity; use Modifier.windowInsetsPadding(WindowInsets.systemBars) for custom layouts; Scaffold handles this automatically
Don't: Hardcode padding for navigation bar height; ignore gesture navigation bar inset (zero on gesture nav but not on 3-button)
Code Bad: Modifier.padding(bottom = 48.dp) // Hardcoded nav bar height breaks on gesture navigation

Guideline: Lift state appropriately — hoist to lowest common ancestor
Description: State defined inside a composable can't be shared with siblings or parents needing it; unhoisted state prevents reuse and testing
Do: Hoist state to nearest common ancestor; pass state down and events (lambdas) up; use ViewModel for screen-level state
Don't: Class-level state in pure composable functions; duplicate state in sibling composables instead of hoisting
Code Good: @Composable fun Screen(viewModel: ScreenViewModel = hiltViewModel()) { val uiState by viewModel.uiState.collectAsState(); Content(state=uiState, onAction=viewModel::handle) }
Code Bad: @Composable fun Screen() { var isLoading by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }; SiblingA(isLoading); SiblingB(isLoading) }  // Duplicated

Guideline: Use ViewModel with StateFlow for screen state
Description: Compose recomposition handles UI updates but state must survive configuration changes; ViewModel + StateFlow provides lifecycle-aware reactable state
Do: Use StateFlow<UiState> in ViewModel; collect with collectAsStateWithLifecycle(); single UiState sealed class/data class
Don't: Use LiveData in new Compose code (prefer StateFlow); multiple flows for different state pieces (merge into UiState)
Code Good: val uiState: StateFlow<HomeUiState> = _uiState.asStateFlow(); @Composable fun Screen(vm: HomeVM) { val state by vm.uiState.collectAsStateWithLifecycle() }
Code Bad: var posts by mutableStateOf(listOf<Post>())  // In composable directly — lost on configuration change

Guideline: Provide contentDescription for images and icons
Description: Icon-only buttons and network images have no accessible name without contentDescription; TalkBack announces 'unlabeled' or file name
Do: contentDescription for informational images and icon buttons; null for decorative images; semantics block for custom roles
Don't: Leave contentDescription null on tappable Icon buttons; use generic 'icon' as description; describe appearance not meaning
Code Bad: Icon(Icons.Filled.Favorite, contentDescription = null)  // Tappable icon with no accessible name

Guideline: Mark headings in semantics for TalkBack navigation
Description: TalkBack supports heading navigation via linear navigation or custom heading navigation; composables without heading role are not navigable as headings
Do: Use Modifier.semantics { heading() } on screen title and section headers; test with TalkBack heading navigation
Don't: Mark every large text as heading (only true headings); use heading for decorative large display text
Code Bad: Text('Your Feed', style=MaterialTheme.typography.headlineSmall)  // Not navigable as heading by TalkBack

Description: Instant visibility changes (if visible else null) are jarring; AnimatedVisibility wraps entry/exit animations declaratively
Do: AnimatedVisibility with customized enter/exit spec; Crossfade for content that changes between states; AnimatedContent for content-changing transitions
Don't: Use visibility modifier instead (element still composed); show/hide with alpha 0 (still receives clicks)
Code Bad: if (isLoaded) Content() else null  // Instant appears no transition

Guideline: Respect system animation scale and reduceMotion
Description: Android developer options allow disabling animations; Compose respects AnimatorDurationScale; additionally respect Accessibility reduceMotion setting
Do: Check LocalReduceMotion or animationsEnabled; use shorter duration or instant for reduceMotion; always support animation scale factor of 0
Don't: Play full animations regardless of system animation scale; use hard-coded durations ignoring scale
Code Good: val reduceMotion = LocalReduceMotion.current; val duration = if (reduceMotion == ReduceMotion.Enabled) 0 else 300; animatedContent(targetState) { target -> transitions based on duration }

Guideline: Use Coil for async image loading in Compose
Description: Coil integrates with Compose naturally; provides AsyncImage composable with lifecycle-aware loading caching and error handling
Do: Use AsyncImage from coil3-compose; provide placeholder and error; set model with context+url; use ContentScale matching image usage
Don't: Manually download bitMap in coroutine and set to Image (no caching no lifecycle); use Glide with traditional view binding in Compose
Code Good: AsyncImage(model = ImageRequest.Builder(context).data(url).crossfade(true).build(), contentDescription = 'Profile photo', contentScale = ContentScale.Crop, placeholder = painterResource(R.drawable.placeholder), error = painterResource(R.drawable.error), modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth())
Code Bad: LaunchedEffect(url) { val bmp = withContext(Dispatchers.IO) { loadBitmap(url) }; bitmap = bmp }  // Manual, no cache

Guideline: Use MaterialTheme tokens for all colors shapes typography
Description: Hardcoded colors break dark mode; non-theme sizes are inconsistent and unmaintainable; theme tokens respond to dynamic color (Android 12+)
Do: Use MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary surface onBackground etc.; MaterialTheme.typography.headlineMedium; MaterialTheme.shapes.medium
Don't: Hardcode Color(0xFFBB86FC) purple; use arbitrary corner radius values not from shapes theme
Code Bad: Surface(color = Color(0xFFFFFFFF)) { Text('Hello', fontSize = 16.sp, color = Color.Black) }  // Not adaptive

Description: Compose UI tests use ComposeTestRule which provides semantic tree querying; allows testing without knowing implementation
Do: Use onNodeWithText() onNodeWithContentDescription() onNodeWithTag(); performClick() performScrollTo(); assertIsDisplayed() assertEquals()
Don't: Use onNodeWithText for implementation details (fragment class names); use index-based queries that break on content changes
Code Good: @Test fun login_success() { rule.onNodeWithText('Sign In').performClick(); rule.onNodeWithText('Welcome, Alice').assertIsDisplayed() }

Guideline: Use sealed classes for UiState to model all screen states
Description: Unmodeled states (loading error empty success) lead to runtime null errors and undefined UI; sealed class forces handling all cases
Do: Sealed class or sealed interface with Loading Success Error Empty states; when expression exhausts all cases; never skip a state
Don't: Use nullable data class with isLoading:Boolean and error:String? (can be in inconsistent combination like isLoading=true AND error!=null)
Code Good: sealed interface HomeUiState { object Loading : HomeUiState; data class Success(val posts: List<Post>) : HomeUiState; data class Error(val message: String) : HomeUiState }; when(state) { is Loading -> LoadingSpinner() is Success -> PostList(state.posts) is Error -> ErrorView(state.message) }
Code Bad: data class HomeUiState(val posts: List<Post>? = null, val isLoading: Boolean = false, val error: String? = null)  // Can be logically inconsistent

Guideline: Use key() to help Compose identify list item identity
Description: Without keys Compose uses position to identify items; insertions/deletions at start recompose all items; keys allow structural diff
Do: Provide key in LazyColumn items(key={it.id}); also in Column with key() recompose block for smaller lists; keys must be stable and unique
Don't: Use index as key (defeats purpose); change key values (triggers full recompose of that item)
Code Good: LazyColumn { items(posts, key = { post -> post.id }) { post -> PostCard(post) } }
Code Bad: LazyColumn { itemsIndexed(posts) { index, post -> PostCard(post) } }  // Index key — insertion at top recomposes all
